entries.definition:
in the Qur’an: عَزَّرَ ʿazzara three times and عُزَيْر ʿuzayr once.
عَزَّرَ ʿazzara [v. II, trans.] 1 to assist (48:9) لتؤمنوا بالله ورسوله
وتعزروه that you may believe in God and His Messenger, and you
may support Him 2 to honour (7:157) فالذين آمنوا به وعزروه ونصروه
so those who believe in him, honour and help him.

عُزَيْر ʿuzayr [proper noun] Ezra (Book of Ezra) (9:30) وقالت اليهود
عزير ابن الله the Jews said, ‘Ezra is the son of God’ (Asad quotes
Muslim sources including ʾal-Ṭabarī as attributing the
worshipping of Ezra to some Jews of Medina).
entries.definition_english:
in the Qur’an: عَزَّرَ ʿazzara three times and عُزَيْر ʿuzayr once.
عَزَّرَ ʿazzara [v. II, trans.] 1 to assist (48:9) لتؤمنوا بالله ورسوله
وتعزروه that you may believe in God and His Messenger, and you
may support Him 2 to honour (7:157) فالذين آمنوا به وعزروه ونصروه
so those who believe in him, honour and help him.

عُزَيْر ʿuzayr [proper noun] Ezra (Book of Ezra) (9:30) وقالت اليهود
عزير ابن الله the Jews said, ‘Ezra is the son of God’ (Asad quotes
Muslim sources including ʾal-Ṭabarī as attributing the
worshipping of Ezra to some Jews of Medina).

entries.definition:
ʿ–z–z power, might, downpour, to be impregnable, to be hard,
to be rough (e.g. land); to overcome; to support, to be near to
one’s heart, to value highly; to be consoled; to cooperate, to be
rare. Of this root, nine forms occur 120 times in the Qur’an: عَزَّ
ʿazza once; عَزَّزَ ʿazzaza once; تُعِزُّ tuʿizzu once; عِزّ ʿizz once; عِزَّة
ʿizzatun 11 times; عَزِيز ʿazīz 99 times; أَعَزّ ʾaʿazz three times; أَعِزَّة
ʾaʿizzatun twice and الْعُزّى ʾal-ʿuzzā once.

عَزَّ ʿazza u [v. trans.] to overcome, to prevail over, to get the
better of (38:23) فقال أكفلنيها وعزني في الخطاب and he said, ‘Put her in
my charge’, and he got the better of me in the argument.

عَزَّزَ ʿazzaza [v. II trans.] to reinforce, to strengthen (36:14)
فكذبوهما فعززنا بثالث but they rejected both, so We reinforced [them]
with a third.

تُعِزُّ tuʿizzu [imperf. of v. IV أَعَزَّ ʾaʿazza] to exalt, to give power
to (3:26) وتعز من تشاء وتذل من تشاء You exalt whomever You will and
abase whomever You will.

عِزّ ʿizz [v. n./n.] support, strength (19:81) ليكونوا لهم عزا that they
might be for them a support.

عِزَّة ʿizzatun I [v. n.] feeling proud of *(2:206) أخذته العزة بالإثم he
was too arrogant to accept [lit. his pride led him to persist in his
sinful ways] II [n.] 1 power, support (4:139) فإن العزة لله جميعا all
power is truly for God 2 glory, exaltation (26:44) بعزة فرعون إنا لنحن
الغالبون by Pharaoh’s might, we shall be victors.

عَزِيز ʿazīz [quasi-act. part./n., pl. أَعِزَّة ʾaʿizzatun] 1 mighty,

618

618
